examples.sentence: During the meeting, Tom tried to impress the boss but ended up making an ass of himself by mixing up the project details.

examples.sentence: If you don't prepare for the presentation, you might make an ass of yourself in front of the entire team.

examples.sentence: He tends to make an ass of himself whenever he tries to tell jokes to strangers.

examples.sentence: Don't make an ass of yourself by arguing with the referee during the game.
examples.translation: Don't behave embarrassingly by arguing with the referee during the game.

examples.sentence: They said he made an ass of himself at the wedding by dancing on the tables.

examples.sentence: The principal decided to make an example of the student who cheated on the exam by suspending him for a month.

examples.sentence: If they make an example of the employee caught stealing, it will send a clear message to everyone else.

examples.translation: The manager publicly reprimanded the latecomer to discourage others from being late.

examples.sentence: The professor made an example of the faulty calculation to clarify the concept for the entire class.

examples.sentence: During training, she made an example of the correct procedure to ensure everyone understood it.
examples.translation: During training, she used the correct procedure as an illustration to ensure everyone understood it.

examples.sentence: He made an example of his own work to show the new hires how the report should be formatted.
examples.translation: He used his own work as an illustration to show the new hires how the report should be formatted.

examples.sentence: The company made an example of the contractor who ignored safety rules by terminating his contract publicly.

examples.sentence: They made an example of the employee who leaked confidential information to discourage others.
examples.translation: They publicly reprimanded the employee who leaked confidential information to discourage others.

examples.translation: The court publicly punished the offender by sentencing him harshly in open court.

examples.sentence: We should be careful not to make an example of someone unjustly, as it could damage reputations unfairly.

examples.sentence: At the family reunion, Jack really made an exhibition of himself by singing loudly off-key.

examples.sentence: She tends to make an exhibition of herself whenever she drinks too much at parties.

examples.sentence: After Sheila became pregnant, John decided to make an honest woman of her and proposed immediately.

examples.sentence: Back in the day, it was common for men to make an honest woman of their partners if they were expecting a child.

examples.sentence: If he really cares, he'll make an honest woman of her instead of just walking away.
examples.translation: If he really cares, he'll marry her instead of just walking away.

examples.translation: They decided to marry each other after five years of dating.

examples.sentence: He made an honest woman out of his longtime girlfriend last summer in a small ceremony.

examples.sentence: She hopes to make an honest woman of him someday, but he hasn't proposed yet.
examples.translation: She hopes to marry him someday, but he hasn't proposed yet.

examples.sentence: We watched them make an honest woman of her at the beautiful outdoor wedding.
examples.translation: We watched them marry her at the beautiful outdoor wedding.

examples.sentence: After the conference, the team decided to make a night of it by going out for dinner and dancing.
examples.translation: After the conference, the team decided to spend the evening out eating and dancing.

examples.sentence: Let's not just have a quick drink; we should make a night of it and enjoy ourselves fully.

examples.sentence: They made a night of it celebrating their anniversary, starting with a fancy meal and ending at a jazz club.

examples.sentence: Making a night of it at the festival means enjoying all the performances and food stalls until late.

examples.sentence: They made a night of it by exploring several bars across town after the concert.

examples.sentence: Her groundbreaking research made a noise in the world of medicine, earning her international acclaim.

examples.sentence: The young entrepreneur made a noise in the world by launching an innovative app that everyone talked about.

examples.sentence: If you want to make a noise in the world, you need to be bold and take risks with your ideas.

examples.sentence: The musician made a noise in the world after his debut album topped the charts.

examples.sentence: They made a noise in the world by organizing the largest charity event in the city.

examples.sentence: Making a noise in the world requires dedication and a unique vision.

examples.sentence: The activist made a noise in the world through passionate speeches and community work.
examples.translation: The activist attracted public attention through passionate speeches and community work.

examples.sentence: At the holiday feast, Jake really made a pig of himself by eating three helpings of every dish.
examples.translation: At the holiday feast, Jake ate greedily by having three helpings of every dish.

examples.sentence: She tends to make a pig of herself whenever there’s free food at the office parties.
examples.translation: She tends to eat greedily whenever there’s free food at the office parties.

examples.sentence: Don’t make a pig of yourself at the buffet; leave some food for others.
examples.translation: Don’t eat greedily at the buffet; leave some food for others.

examples.sentence: He made a pig of himself by refusing to share and grabbing all the snacks for himself.
examples.translation: He behaved selfishly by refusing to share and grabbing all the snacks for himself.

examples.sentence: Nobody likes it when someone makes a pig of themselves at the dinner table, acting rude and messy.

examples.sentence: Stop making a pig of yourself and show some manners while eating with others.

examples.sentence: He made a pig of himself by eating way more than what’s considered healthy at the party.

examples.sentence: If you keep making a pig of yourself during meals, people might start avoiding inviting you.
examples.translation: If you keep overindulging during meals, people might start avoiding inviting you.

examples.sentence: She realized she had made a pig of herself at the banquet and promised to eat more moderately next time.

examples.sentence: The kids made a pig of themselves at the picnic, stuffing their faces with sandwiches and sweets.
examples.translation: The kids ate greedily at the picnic, stuffing their faces with sandwiches and sweets.

examples.sentence: At the barbecue, everyone made a pig of themselves, enjoying the feast to the fullest.

examples.sentence: Don’t make a pig of yourself at the buffet; pace yourself and eat moderately.

examples.sentence: He made a pig's ear of the presentation by forgetting half his slides and stumbling over his words.

examples.sentence: If you keep making a pig's ear of your assignments, you might fail the course.

examples.sentence: They accused the new staff member of making a pig's ear of the project by missing critical deadlines.

examples.sentence: She really made a pig's ear of the repairs, so now the sink leaks even worse than before.

examples.sentence: Don’t make a pig's ear of the report; take your time to double-check all the facts.

examples.sentence: Don’t make a pig's ear of the paperwork or the application might be rejected.

examples.sentence: During the debate, Maria made a point about climate change that left everyone thoughtful.

examples.sentence: I want to make a point that honesty is more important than success in any job.

examples.sentence: To make a point about punctuality, the teacher started the class exactly on time every day.

examples.translation: She deliberately drew attention to positive feedback during the meeting to boost morale.

examples.sentence: She made a run for it just before the deadline to submit her application on time.
examples.translation: She dashed quickly to submit her application just before the deadline.

examples.sentence: When the opportunity arose, they made a run for it to secure the best seats in the theater.
examples.translation: When the opportunity arose, they sprinted quickly to secure the best seats in the theater.

examples.sentence: He made a run for it across the field to catch the bus before it left the station.
examples.translation: He ran quickly across the field to catch the bus before it left the station.

examples.sentence: The children made a run for it as soon as the school bell rang.
examples.translation: The children ran quickly to leave as soon as the school bell rang.

examples.sentence: He made a run for it to catch the last train home yesterday evening.
examples.translation: He ran quickly to catch the last train home yesterday evening.

examples.sentence: If you hear a suspicious noise, make a run for it and find a safe place immediately.
examples.translation: If you hear a suspicious noise, run quickly and find a safe place immediately.

examples.sentence: When she found out about the betrayal, she made a scene in front of all their friends.
examples.translation: When she found out about the betrayal, she caused a public disturbance in front of all their friends.

examples.sentence: Please don’t make a scene at the restaurant over such a minor mistake.

examples.sentence: He made a scene at the airport when his flight was delayed for several hours.
examples.translation: He caused a public disturbance at the airport when his flight was delayed for several hours.

examples.sentence: Don’t make a scene just because you didn’t get your way; it’s not worth it.
